items.summary: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.

items.title: US Senate Candidate Caught Insider Trading on Kalshi Says He Did It on Purpose

items.title: Cocaine-Fueled Wild Salmon Swam Twice as Far as Sober Ones
items.summary: After scientists exposed wild fish to cocaine and a cocaine metabolite, they observed that, as in the lab, fish on cocaine do not act like normal fish.

items.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
items.summary: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

items.summary: The admission could open Tesla to legal challenges after it spent years promising customers they were just one software update away from owning fully autonomous cars.

items.summary: Such a move would provide liquidity in dollars to the oil-rich UAE, but could be politically tenuous for the administration amid higher prices at home.

items.summary: Volkswagen revealed four cars, including the ID. UNYX 09, which the company said it co-developed with EV maker Xpeng in two years.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Wind, solar energy support slips: Survey
items.summary: A new Gallup poll released Wednesday showed that support for wind and solar energy has slipped, with a majority still favoring those energy alternatives. The poll found that 66 percent of Americans prefer the U.S. to put more emphasis on solar energy and 55 percent prefer the same with wind energy. Both dropped from Gallup’s…

items.title: Democrats fume over lack of Oversight hearings, accusing GOP of dodging on Epstein
items.summary: House Oversight Committee Democrats are fuming over Republicans’ shift away from holding formal hearings, accusing the majority of trying to dodge any further action on subpoenas in the Epstein investigation and questioning whether the order came from the White House. Instead of hearings, where witnesses are sworn in and members can make motions, the panel…

items.title: Changes loom for National Weather Service as union official warns of potential staff cuts
items.summary: The Trump administration is reorganizing the National Weather Service (NWS) and making changes to forecasting, while a key union representative warns the reorganization could involve potential staff cuts. Federal employees at the NWS’s Weather Forecast Offices, which provide people with local forecasts and severe weather warnings, have learned about impending changes to their baseline staffing…

items.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
items.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to understanding disease risk. It may even lead to future screening tests that detect cancer risk earlier.

items.title: A simple blood test could reveal Alzheimer’s risk years early
items.summary: A routine blood marker tied to inflammation may reveal Alzheimer’s risk years in advance. Scientists found that higher neutrophil levels—part of the body’s first immune response—were linked to a greater chance of developing dementia. The discovery suggests this common lab value could help flag at-risk individuals before symptoms appear. It also raises the possibility that immune cells themselves may be fueling the disease.

items.title: Scientists stunned as JWST finds ice clouds on a giant alien planet
items.summary: Scientists have discovered unexpected water-ice clouds on a distant, Jupiter-like exoplanet, challenging current atmospheric models. By directly imaging Epsilon Indi Ab with the James Webb Space Telescope, they found less ammonia than expected—likely hidden by thick, patchy clouds. The finding reveals new layers of complexity in giant planets and shows how much we still have to learn.

items.title: An experimental new drug for stiff person syndrome restores mobility
items.summary: CAR T cell therapy wipes out rogue antibodies' source and improves walking speed in people with the same autoimmune disorder that affects Celine Dion.

items.title: Suicide deaths in U.S. teens and young adults fell after 988 launch
items.summary: Suicide is a top cause of death for teens and young adults. A study finds a link between the 988 Lifeline and a drop in their suicide mortality.

items.title: How climate change may increase antibiotic resistance
items.summary: Rising heat and drought may spur bacteria to exchange antibiotic resistance genes, with potential risks to human health.
